content.text: Workspaces are the primary unit of resource isolation in a Claude organisation. Each workspace gets its own API keys, member assignments, and optional token-usage quotas — nothing is shared across workspace boundaries. Managing workspaces through the Admin API lets you provision team environments automatically, enforce naming conventions, cap spend per team, and decommission environments that are no longer needed without touching the Console UI.

attrs.alt: Workspace hierarchy and resource isolation model

content.text: A workspace belongs to exactly one organisation. Within a workspace you control:
(which grant inference access for that workspace),
(users may belong to multiple workspaces with different roles), and
(an optional monthly token cap that blocks inference once exceeded).
The workspace object exposed by the API has these core fields:

. Archiving disables all API keys in the workspace so no inference can run, but retains all data (audit logs, member list, usage history). You can unarchive at any time. Deletion is permanent and should be reserved for test workspaces with no audit-relevant history.
Deletion permanently removes usage history and audit records. Archive instead — archiving costs nothing and preserves the paper trail.
. Names must be unique within the organisation.

import anthropic, os

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

workspace = client.admin.workspaces.create(
    display_name="ml-research",
    description="Workspace for ML research team — GPU-intensive workloads"
)

print(workspace.id)            # wkspc_01XYZ...
print(workspace.display_name)  # ml-research
print(workspace.archived_at)   # None  (active)
For large rollouts — onboarding many teams at once — script the creation loop with a structured config rather than manual Console clicks.

TEAMS = [
    {"name": "fraud-detection",  "desc": "Fraud ML team"},
    {"name": "customer-success", "desc": "CS automation"},
    {"name": "legal-review",     "desc": "Contract analysis"},
]

workspaces = {}
for team in TEAMS:
    ws = client.admin.workspaces.create(
        display_name=team["name"],
        description=team["desc"]
    )
    workspaces[team["name"]] = ws.id
    print(f"Created {ws.display_name}: {ws.id}")

# workspaces is now a name -> id map for downstream use

# List all workspaces (active + archived), paginated
def get_all_workspaces(client):
    workspaces = []
    after = None
    while True:
        page = client.admin.workspaces.list(
            limit=100,
            **({'after': after} if after else {})
        )
        workspaces.extend(page.data)
        if not page.has_more:
            break
        after = page.last_id
    return workspaces

all_ws = get_all_workspaces(client)
active   = [w for w in all_ws if w.archived_at is None]
archived = [w for w in all_ws if w.archived_at is not None]
print(f"{len(active)} active, {len(archived)} archived workspaces")
To retrieve a single workspace by ID, use
. This is faster than scanning the full list when you already hold a workspace ID from a config store or environment variable.
to rename, update the description, or toggle the archived state. Only send the fields you want to change — this is a partial-update endpoint.

# Rename a workspace
client.admin.workspaces.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    display_name="ml-research-v2"
)

# Archive (disable keys, preserve data)
client.admin.workspaces.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    archive=True
)

# Unarchive (re-enable — keys remain disabled, must re-create)
client.admin.workspaces.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    archive=False
)
Archiving revokes all API keys in the workspace. When you unarchive, the workspace becomes active again but has no keys — you must create new ones. Plan for this in your unarchive runbook.
A user can belong to multiple workspaces simultaneously, with independent roles in each. Workspace-level roles are
. Organisation-level admins automatically have full access to all workspaces.
Returns user ID, email, workspace role
User must already be an org member
Removes from workspace only, not org

# Add a user to a workspace with developer role
client.admin.workspaces.members.create(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz",
    workspace_role="workspace_developer"
)

# Promote to workspace admin
client.admin.workspaces.members.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz",
    workspace_role="workspace_admin"
)

# Remove from workspace (user stays in org)
client.admin.workspaces.members.delete(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz"
)
Sync workspace members from an HRIS or IdP
For teams managed in an identity provider (Okta, Azure AD), you can mirror group membership into workspace membership with a nightly sync job.
You can attach a monthly token-spend cap to a workspace via the
endpoint. Once the cap is hit, inference calls from that workspace return a
until the monthly window resets. Limits are expressed in

# Set a 5 million token/month cap on a workspace
client.admin.workspaces.usage_limits.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    monthly_token_limit=5_000_000
)

# Read current limit
limit = client.admin.workspaces.usage_limits.retrieve(workspace_id="wkspc_01abc")
print(limit.monthly_token_limit)

# Remove the cap (unlimited)
client.admin.workspaces.usage_limits.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    monthly_token_limit=None
)
Usage limit enforcement has a small lag — the API does not block instantly on hitting the cap. Design applications to handle
at any time and surface informative messages to end users rather than failing silently.
Environment provisioning on new project creation
in project metadata for future API calls.

import anthropic, os, boto3

def provision_project_workspace(project_slug: str, team_user_ids: list[str],
                                 monthly_token_limit: int):
    client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])
    ssm    = boto3.client("ssm")

    # 1. Create workspace
    ws = client.admin.workspaces.create(display_name=project_slug)

    # 2. Create inference key
    key = client.admin.api_keys.create(
        workspace_id=ws.id, name=f"{project_slug}-key"
    )
    ssm.put_parameter(
        Name=f"/projects/{project_slug}/claude-api-key",
        Value=key.key, Type="SecureString", Overwrite=True
    )
    ssm.put_parameter(
        Name=f"/projects/{project_slug}/claude-workspace-id",
        Value=ws.id, Type="String", Overwrite=True
    )

    # 3. Usage limit
    client.admin.workspaces.usage_limits.update(
        workspace_id=ws.id, monthly_token_limit=monthly_token_limit
    )

    # 4. Add members
    for uid in team_user_ids:
        client.admin.workspaces.members.create(
            workspace_id=ws.id, user_id=uid,
            workspace_role="workspace_developer"
        )

    print(f"Provisioned workspace {ws.id} for {project_slug}")
    return ws.id

from datetime import datetime, timezone

def decommission_workspace(client, workspace_id: str, dry_run: bool = True):
    """Archive a workspace: disable keys, preserve data."""
    ws = client.admin.workspaces.retrieve(workspace_id=workspace_id)
    print(f"Target: {ws.display_name} ({workspace_id})")

    # List active keys
    keys = client.admin.api_keys.list(workspace_id=workspace_id).data
    active_keys = [k for k in keys if not k.disabled_at]
    print(f"  {len(active_keys)} active key(s) will be disabled by archiving")

    if dry_run:
        print("  DRY RUN — no changes made")
        return

    client.admin.workspaces.update(workspace_id=workspace_id, archive=True)
    print(f"  Archived at {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}")
Choose a unique name; append env suffix like -prod
Use sk-ant-admin01- key, not inference key
List workspaces to confirm the ID
Use None to remove limit; positive integer to set it
Adding user not yet in org
Invite user to org first, then add to workspace
Workspaces isolate API keys, members, and usage limits within an organisation. The Admin API exposes
to rename or archive, and nested

sub-resources. Automate environment provisioning by combining workspace creation, key creation, usage-limit setting, and member sync into a single idempotent function. Always archive rather than delete to preserve audit history, and handle the post-unarchive state (no keys) explicitly in your runbooks.
